name: Mux Video API
what_it_does: Developer-first video platform that handles video upload, transcoding, adaptive streaming, and live streaming with best-in-class DX and data analytics.
use_cases: Uploading and streaming user-generated video content in apps
Building live streaming features with low latency
Generating video thumbnails and animated GIFs from agents
not_for: Teams that need self-hosted video infrastructure
Very small projects sensitive to per-minute encoding costs
best_when: You need a fully managed video pipeline with excellent DX, reliable playback, and detailed analytics without managing encoding infrastructure.
avoid_when: You only need basic video hosting without analytics, or have strict data residency requirements Mux can't meet.

auth.notes: Token ID + Token Secret (Basic Auth). Separate environments for test and production. Signing secrets for webhook verification.

pricing.paid_tiers: Encoding: $0.015/min video encoded; Streaming: $0.0030/min delivered; Storage: $0.0060/GB/month
pricing.notes: No free tier but has generous free trial credits. Pricing is transparent and per-minute. Costs can add up for high-volume video apps.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear JSON error responses with type and message fields. HTTP status codes are semantically correct.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Asset and live stream creation are not idempotent. No idempotency key support. Agents must track created resource IDs.
known_agent_gotchas: Video processing is asynchronous - asset status must be polled or tracked via webhooks before playback URL is ready
Direct upload URLs expire after 12 hours - agents must generate fresh upload URLs for each upload
Live streams have a distinct lifecycle (idle, active, disconnected) that agents must handle
No idempotency keys - duplicate upload requests will create duplicate assets
Playback IDs must be created separately from assets for public streaming

security_score_components.security_notes: Access token ID + secret key pair. Signed playback URLs for content protection. SOC2 Type II. Video content is hosted on Mux infrastructure. Webhook signature verification for event validation.
